title: Local File Trigger is disabled in n8n v2
symptom: Users are unable to access the Local File Trigger node in n8n, leading to confusion about its availability.
applies_when: Trying to access Local File Trigger node
not_this_if: Using an earlier version of n8n
Local File Trigger is enabled in configuration
root_cause: In n8n v2, certain nodes, including the Local File Trigger, are disabled by default due to security concerns. This is to prevent unauthorized access to the file system and execution of arbitrary commands.
fix_steps: Open your n8n Docker configuration file
Change NODES_EXCLUDE from its current value to '[]' or remove the specific nodes you want to enable
Restart the n8n service to apply changes
agent_instruction: Update the NODES_EXCLUDE variable in your Docker configuration to enable the Local File Trigger.
safety_notes: Enabling these nodes may expose your system to security risks; ensure you understand the implications.
